content.full_text:
May 2, 1991

Diane M. Fishburn
Olson, Connelly, Hagel,
Fong & Leidigh
300 Capitol Mall, Ste. 350
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Confirmation of Telephone Advice
Our File No. A-91-210

Dear Ms. Fishburn:

On behalf of Patricia Garamendi, you have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided to you concerning the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1

Your letter correctly summarizes my advice. Pursuant to Section 84200.8(c), when a runoff election is held within 60 days of the qualifying election, candidates being voted upon in the runoff election and their controlled committees are required to file one preelection campaign statement in connection with the runoff election.

In the case of the special election for the 26th Assembly District, the qualifying election was held on March 19, 1991, and the runoff election is scheduled for May 14, 1991, which is within 60 days of the qualifying election. Therefore, Ms. Garamendi and her controlled committee(s) must file one preelection campaign statement no later than May 2, 1991, covering the period March 3, 1991 through April 27, 1991.

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

A copy of your letter is enclosed for your convenience. If you have additional questions or need further assistance, please contact me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Carla Wardlow
Assistant Division Chief
Technical Assistance & Analysis Division

Enclosure

cc: Secretary of State
San Joaquin County Registrar of Voters
